title: RA.1029 Valentina Magaletti

translation.content: Valentina Magaletti at the drums is a picture of freedom: laughing, loose-limbed, entirely absorbed. For RA.1029, the London-based percussionist channels that instinct into a rare solo outing—a personal excursion through her musical archive. Its atmosphere moves as freely as she plays, shifting from ominous and claustrophobic passages to contemplative field recordings. Taught by a jazz musician in her hometown of Bari, Magaletti has become one of electronic music's most distinctive drummers. She plays in a particular way: impish, contained and precise, yet also unruly. Being pinned down to one outfit or sound isn't on her agenda. The closest thing to a throughline across her work is an instinct for summoning music's wilder edges. Collaboration is one of the central ways she continually reinvents herself, whether it be spiritual dub excursions with Shackleton and Holy Tongue, or post-punk melancholia with Moin. As she told the Guardian in 2024, "dialogue is more interesting than monologue." Take her work with Princípe associate Nídia, in which she used Angolan kuduro as a springboard for new acoustic visions of dance music. But Magaletti is also a solo artist in her own right, and RA.1029 is the sound of her own monologue. The 90-minute mix sees her roving, from wild drum excursions and Midwestern industrial to frenetic free jazz, eerie gqom and Ukrainian electro. It captures, she says, her current inner state, a feeling of being "suspended between introspection and anticipation." Fitting, then, for a groundbreaking artist who thrives in the spaces in between. –Bella Aquilina What have you been up to recently? I'm coming down from what felt like an absolutely insane year of touring and back-to-back collaborations. It was intense, rewarding and creatively expansive, but also quite demanding. Right now I'm in a recovery phase processing everything that happened, letting things settle, and allowing myself the space to recalibrate after being constantly in motion. How and where was the mix recorded? And can you tell us the idea behind it? The mix came together in a very personal, almost archival way. I digitised parts of my record collection and blended them with a few unreleased sounds, old sessions and sound collages I've been sitting on for a while. Everything was assembled and mastered in Logic. Conceptually, the mix reflects where I am emotionally right now. I feel quite grounded, almost in hibernation mode. The selections are close to my current inner state, there's a sense of being both slightly deluded and deeply excited at the same time, suspended between introspection and anticipation. What are you looking forward to in the near future? I'm really looking forward to making more music, but in a slower, more intentional way. I want to absorb things more fully,listening closely, being present, and appreciating small moments. Spending time with my record collection, going on long walks, and letting ideas unfold naturally feels more important to me than rushing into the next thing. What's one social or political cause you want the world to pay more attention to? I want to be more present and active for the LGBTQ+ community. Being there for my people feels essential, especially at a time when we are increasingly exposed to unfairness, hostility, and discrimination. Visibility, care, and solidarity matter more than ever, and I want my presence both personally and creatively to reflect that. What's one club or party that had a major impact on you as an artist? I'll be a bit conventional here and say Cafe OTO. I've been connected to it from day one, and it has played a huge role in shaping how I listen, perform and think about music. It truly means a lot to me. I also have to mention VESPER, a brand-new queer club in South London. I'm a shareholder, and it represents something deeply important to me not just as a space for music, but as a community-driven, queer-led project with real intent and energy behind it.
translation.blurb: The singular percussionist turns inward for a rare solo excursion.
